title: Bitter Chewy Filipino Tempeh Stir-fry
description: A plant-based Filipino stir-fry featuring chewy tempeh, bitter amaranth leaves, and a bright orange zest glaze for an energizing bite.
instructions: Prepare all ingredients and keep them within easy reach.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the minced garlic, grated ginger, and diced onion; stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until aromatic.
Add the tempeh cubes and sauté until they turn golden brown on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced bell pepper and continue cooking for another 2 minutes.
Add the bitter amaranth leaves and stir until they wilt slightly, about 1 minute.
Deglaze the pan by pouring in the lime juice and a splash of water, scraping up any browned bits with a wooden spoon.
Stir in the fermented soy sauce, sweet soy sauce, and orange zest, letting the flavors meld for 2 minutes.
If desired, sprinkle in toasted rice flour and a little water to thicken the sauce; cook for 1 more minute.
Taste the stir-fry and adjust seasoning if needed. Serve hot over steamed rice or as a side dish. Enjoy the bitter, chewy, and energizing flavors!
